ideas.title: Worker Desk Glow Streak Ring
ideas.description: Each desk gets a subtle colored halo that intensifies on consecutive completions (warm amber) and cools to icy blue after errors. WHY: performance is currently only readable by checking stats — a persistent ambient glow lets you feel team momentum without scanning numbers.

ideas.title: Idle Worker Screensaver Doodles
ideas.description: When a worker's status is idle, their desk screen switches from the work SVG to a tiny animated screensaver (flying toasters, bouncing shapes, starfield). WHY: idle and active states look too similar right now; a distinct screensaver makes it instantly obvious who's waiting for work.

ideas.title: Bloberto Thought Bubble Quips
ideas.description: Bloberto occasionally displays a small thought bubble with a reactive one-liner about the current team state (e.g., '3 errors… fine.' or 'Full squad! 🎉'). Triggered by state changes, shown briefly then fades. WHY: Bloberto is the visual anchor of the whole dashboard but currently static — giving him inner monologue makes the mascot earn his desk space.

ideas.title: Desk Plants That Grow and Wilt
ideas.description: Each worker gets a tiny potted plant on their desk. It grows greener and taller as their success rate climbs over the session and slowly wilts if they rack up errors. WHY: long-term performance needs a visual memory — plants encode history in a warm, non-judgmental way that fits the office vibe better than graphs or numbers.

ideas.title: Corner Vibe Lava Lamp
ideas.description: A decorative lava lamp in the office corner that changes blob rise-speed and color temperature based on the current team vibe key. Chill vibe = slow purple blobs; chaotic vibe = fast red blobs. WHY: the vibe system already drives whiteboard quotes but has no physical presence in the room — a lava lamp is ambient data viz that doesn't demand attention.

ideas.title: Conference Table Coffee Cup Pipeline Gauge
ideas.description: A row of coffee cups sits on the conference table. Cups fill as tasks queue up and empty as workers complete them. A stack of empty cups = team is crushing it; cups overflowing = backlog warning. WHY: pipeline pressure is invisible in the current UI — coffee cups are a playful, legible metaphor that fits the office theme perfectly.

ideas.title: End-of-Shift Countdown Wall Clock
ideas.description: A wall clock shows a stylized countdown to a configurable 'end of shift' time. As it ticks down, the clock face color shifts from calm green to urgent orange-red. Workers subtly start gathering their things in the final minutes. WHY: the dashboard exists outside time — there's no sense of urgency or rhythm. A countdown clock gives the whole office a shared heartbeat and makes long sessions feel alive.
